tableRow.maxContractTerm: No statutory maximum found. Florida Education Code (Ch. 1006 Subpart E; Ch. 1010 Part I) and the State Board of Education school-district purchasing rule (Fla. Admin. Code R. 6A-1.012) impose no maximum term on district school-board contracts with private student-transportation contractors. The Ch. 287 procurement caps (e.g., § 287.057(13)-(14)) apply to 'agencies' as defined in § 287.012 (state executive-branch units), not to district school boards.
tableRow.rebidRequired: Required. Under Fla. Admin. Code R. 6A-1.012(7), school districts must request competitive solicitations from three or more sources for any authorized commodities or contractual services exceeding $50,000, and districts may not divide a procurement to avoid the threshold. 2001 Florida AG Informal Opinion (Duval County) confirms the rule reaches school-bus transportation contracts. Exceptions: cooperative/piggyback, sole-source (after 7-business-day public notice), and emergency procurement (superintendent's written finding).
tableRow.renewalMechanics: Statute silent. Neither Fla. Stat. Ch. 1006 Subpart E nor Fla. Admin. Code R. 6A-1.012 / R. 6A-3.0171 prescribes a renewal mechanism for school-district student-transportation contracts. Districts establish renewal terms in their own RFPs and contracts. Rule 6A-1.012 permits piggyback purchases from other governmental agencies' contracts in lieu of competitive solicitation.
tableRow.statutoryCitation: Fla. Admin. Code R. 6A-1.012, adopted under § 1010.04(4)(a), Fla. Stat.; §§ 1006.21, 1006.22, 1006.27, Fla. Stat. (2024); Fla. AGO Informal Op. of Apr. 25, 2001 (Duval County).
tableRow.districtVariance: Yes — variance exists in district contracting practice (term length, renewal options, escalators), not in interpretation of the underlying statutory rule. All 67 districts apply the same R. 6A-1.012 $50,000 threshold; the 2001 AGO is statewide guidance.
